migrations.description: Migrate legacy Query type to new query hierarchy (SimpleRtQuery, AggregationRtQuery, GroupingAggregationRtQuery)

scripts.description:
Migration from System 1.0.3 to 2.0.0.

This migration transforms all legacy Query entities to the new SimpleRtQuery type.
The new query hierarchy introduces:
- PersistentQuery: Abstract base type for all queries
- SimpleRtQuery: For row/tuple queries with filtering, sorting, and column selection
- AggregationRtQuery: For aggregation queries (Count, Min, Max, Avg, Sum)
- GroupingAggregationRtQuery: For grouped aggregation queries with GROUP BY

steps.description: Change CkTypeId from Query to SimpleRtQuery for all existing Query entities
Add empty Description attribute to migrated queries

postValidations.description: Ensure no entities with legacy Query type exist after migration
Verify that SimpleRtQuery entities were created

types.description: Query type for performing aggregation operations (Count, Min, Max, Avg, Sum) on entity data without grouping.
Persistent stream-data query that performs aggregation operations (Count, Min, Max, Avg, Sum) on time-series data without grouping.
Manages auto-incrementing sequences for generating unique identifiers with configurable format and range.
Snapshot of seed entities captured immediately before a blueprint apply, used to support rollback.
Append-only audit log of blueprint apply operations in the tenant. One entity per apply (install, update, rollback, re-apply).
Records a blueprint that is currently installed in the tenant. One entity per installed blueprint; replaced on update or re-apply, removed on uninstall.
Base type for all configuration types

Persistent stream-data query that downsamples time-series data using DATE_BIN bucketing with per-column aggregation (AVG, MIN, MAX). From, To, and Limit (bucket count) are required at the API level even though the schema marks them optional on the base.
Abstract base type for all entities in the system. Provides common indexing and association capabilities.
Query type for performing grouped aggregation operations with GROUP BY functionality on entity data.
Persistent stream-data query that performs grouped aggregation with GROUP BY functionality on time-series data.
Records executed CK model migrations per tenant. Each instance represents one migration execution from a source version to a target version.
Abstract base type for all query types. Defines common query properties like name, description, and target type.
Query type for retrieving rows/tuples from entities with support for column selection, sorting, and filtering.
Persistent stream-data query that retrieves time-series rows for a given CK type with optional column projection, sorting, and field filters.
Abstract base for stream-data query types. Carries the common time-range, limit, rtId scope, archive reference, and field-filter attributes shared by all concrete SdQuery subtypes.
Represents a tenant in the multi-tenant system with its unique identifier, parent relationship, and database mapping.
Configuration for a tenant regarding data version of schema or identity data
Configuration for a tenant if the tenant is in a specific mode, e.g. test mode or maintenance mode

associationRoles.description: Hierarchical parent-child relationship for building tree structures.
Generic many-to-many relationship for linking related entities.

attributes.description: Defines an aggregation column with its attribute path and aggregation type.
Defines a set of aggregation columns for the query, with its attribute path and aggregation type.
Type of aggregation function to apply (Count, Min, Max, Avg, Sum).
Path to an attribute, supporting nested paths with dot notation.
Array of attribute paths for multi-attribute operations.
The current value of the auto-increment counter.
The upper limit of the auto-increment sequence.
Optional format string for generating formatted identifiers (e.g., 'INV-{0:D6}').
Name of the blueprint whose pre-apply state this backup captures.
Version of the blueprint at the time the backup was taken.
Timestamp when this backup was created (before the corresponding apply).
Serialized snapshot of seed entities (and their attributes) that existed before the apply, used to support rollback.
How the blueprint was applied: Install, Update, Rollback, or Reapply.
Timestamp when the apply operation completed.
Name of the blueprint involved in this history entry.
Version of the blueprint involved in this history entry.
Number of seed entities created by this apply.
Number of seed entities deleted by this apply.
Number of seed entities updated by this apply.
Name of the previously installed blueprint version, if this entry represents an update or rollback.
Previously installed version, if this entry represents an update or rollback.
Checksum of the seed-data files applied in this operation.
Name of the installed blueprint (without version).
Semantic version of the installed blueprint.
Timestamp when the blueprint was first installed in this tenant.
True when this blueprint was installed transitively to satisfy another blueprint's dependency, false when it was requested explicitly.
Timestamp of the most recent apply (install, update, re-apply, or rollback).
Full blueprint identifiers (Name-Version) of dependencies that were resolved and installed alongside this blueprint.
Checksum of the seed-data files applied at the most recent apply, used to detect drift on re-apply.
The value to compare against in filter operations.
Stores configuration data such as schema version or identity information.
Name of the database associated with this tenant.
Further information on the item.
Human-readable display name shown in user interfaces.
Indicates whether the entity is active and operational.
Defines the operational mode of the tenant (Development, Testing, Staging, Production).
Comparison operator for field filtering (Equals, Like, GreaterThan, etc.).
Inclusive start of a TimeRange (UTC).
Defines the maintenance state of the tenant (Off, UserApps, FullSystem).
Identifier of the backup created before migration, if any.
Name of the CK model that was migrated (e.g., 'System').
Duration of the migration in milliseconds.
Number of entities added during migration.
Total number of entities affected by the migration.
Number of entities deleted during migration.
Number of entities updated during migration.
List of error messages if migration failed or had issues.
Timestamp when the migration was executed.
Source version of the CK model before migration.
Indicates whether the migration completed successfully.
Target version of the CK model after migration.
List of warning messages generated during migration.
Unique identifier name for the entity.
Identifier of the parent tenant for hierarchical tenant structures.
Search filter applied to specific attributes only.
The Construction Kit type ID that this query targets.
Array of attribute paths to include in the query result.
Array of field filters to apply conditions on specific attributes.
Array of attribute paths to group results by in aggregation queries.
Controls how navigation properties affect the result set.
Array of sort specifications defining the order of query results.
Full-text search filter applied across searchable fields.
Legacy query type selector. Deprecated - use specific query types instead.
Timestamp when the blueprint was applied to create or update this entity.
If true, the entity is managed by the blueprint and will be updated when the blueprint is updated. If false, user modifications are preserved.
Identifies the blueprint that created this entity (format: 'BlueprintName-Version'). Null if entity was created manually.
Sort direction for ordering results (Ascending, Descending, Default).
Runtime id of the CkArchive (concept §3) this query targets at execution time. Stored as OctoObjectId string.
Array of attribute paths to project from the archive table.
Start of the time window for the stream data query. Can be overridden at execution time.
Maximum number of rows to return.
Optional list of runtime entity IDs to scope the stream data query.
End of the time window for the stream data query. Can be overridden at execution time.
Unique identifier for the tenant in the multi-tenant system.
A time range with an inclusive start and exclusive end date (UTC).
Exclusive end of a TimeRange (UTC).

records.description: Defines an aggregation column specifying which attribute to aggregate and how.
Defines a search filter that searches only within specified attributes.
Defines a filter condition for a specific attribute with an operator and comparison value.
Specifies the sorting configuration for a single attribute in query results.
Defines a full-text search filter that searches across all searchable fields.

A time range with an inclusive start and exclusive end date (UTC). System-scope mirror of Basic/TimeRange so System.*-level CKs can reference it without depending on the Basic CK; see concept-time-range-archives.md.

values.name: Count
values.description: Counts the number of items

values.name: Minimum
values.description: Finds the minimum value

values.name: Maximum
values.description: Finds the maximum value

values.name: Average
values.description: Calculates the average value

values.name: Sum
values.description: Calculates the sum of values

enums.description: This enum defines the different aggregation types available for queries.

values.name: Development
values.description: The tenant is in development mode, used for development and testing

values.name: Testing
values.description: The tenant is in testing mode, used for quality assurance

values.name: Staging
values.description: The tenant is in staging mode, used for pre-production testing

values.name: Production
values.description: The tenant is in production mode, used for live operations

enums.description: This enum defines the different modes a tenant can operate in.

values.name: Equals
values.description: Compares the specified field to the specified value.

values.name: NotEquals
values.description: Compares the specified field to the specified value and returns true if the values are not equal.

values.name: LessThan
values.description: Compares the specified field to the specified value and returns true if the field value is less than the specified value.

values.name: LessEqualThan
values.description: Compares the specified field to the specified value and returns true if the field value is less than or equal to the specified value.

values.name: GreaterThan
values.description: Compares the specified field to the specified value and returns true if the field value is greater than the specified value.

values.name: GreaterEqualThan
values.description: Compares the specified field to the specified value and returns true if the field value is greater than or equal to the specified value.

values.name: In
values.description: Compares a field to be equal any value in the specified array.

values.name: NotIn
values.description: Compares a field to be not equal any value in the specified array.

values.name: Like
values.description: Compares a field to the specified value using a pattern matching comparison. Use * as a wildcard character.

values.name: MatchRegEx
values.description: Matches a field containing a value that matches the specified regular expression.

values.name: AnyEq
values.description: Compares an array field with at least one element that matches the specified value.

values.name: AnyLike
values.description: Compares an array field with at least one element that matches the specified value using a pattern matching comparison. Use * as a wildcard character.

values.name: Match
values.description: Matches an array field with at least one element that matches all the specified query criteria.

enums.description: The operator to use for filtering fields.

values.name: Off
values.description: The maintenance mode is off, the tenant is fully operational

values.name: UserApps
values.description: The user apps are in maintenance mode, the tenant is operational but user apps are not available

values.name: FullSystem
values.description: The full system is in maintenance mode, the tenant is not operational

enums.description: This enum defines the maintenance level of a tenant.

values.name: Filter
values.description: Entities without matching associations are filtered out (pre-pagination).

values.name: Include
values.description: Entities without matching associations are kept with null values (post-pagination).

enums.description: Controls how navigation properties affect the result set.

values.name: Flat
values.description: A flat query

values.name: Tree
values.description: A tree query that returns results from a tree

values.name: Default
values.description: Default sorting based on data source type

values.name: Ascending
values.description: Ascending order

values.name: Descending
values.description: Descending order

enums.description: The sort order of a field.

description: Core system model library providing foundational types for entity management, multi-tenant configuration, auto-increment sequences, a comprehensive query system with support for filtering/sorting/aggregations/text-search, the System/TimeRange record used by stream-data archives, and per-tenant blueprint registry types (Installation/History/Backup) that track which blueprints have been applied.
